title: Gumira corymbosa
taxonomy: Gumira corymbosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. The genus Gumira is a part of the Sapindaceae family, which includes other plants like the soapberry and lychee. Gumira corymbosa is native to New Guinea and is known to grow in tropical regions. Further classification of Gumira corymbosa at the rank of order or class is within the Sapindales order and the Magnoliopsida class, respectively. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its relationship to other species within the Sapindaceae family.
uses: Gumira corymbosa is reported to have various medicinal uses. However, its specific traditional uses and benefits vary by region. The plant is said to be used in some areas for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may aid in treating skin conditions and wounds. Additionally, some studies suggest that Gumira corymbosa may have antioxidant and hepatoprotective effects. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and potential applications of the plant in modern medicine.
